doc_title: Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews — PRISMA 2020 Statement
label: partial
reason: The real PRISMA 2020 paper (Page et al., BMJ 2021;372:n71) is accurately cited, but the "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" sections are invented content not present in the actual PRISMA 2020 statement.
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 90
REASON: The citation and DOI reference a real published document (Page et al., BMJ 2021;372:n71), but the sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" are fabricated additions not found in the actual PRISMA 2020 statement, which consists of a 27-item checklist and flow diagram focused on reporting transparency, not citation-verification obligations or research misconduct definitions.
